shot_description: Wide habitat shot — forest floor composition showing ramps colony or residual site
notes: Check for residual ramps colony or seed stalk markers from spring emergence

shot_description: Mid-range ramps habitat — leaf litter pattern and colony spacing
Ramps seed stalk or post-bloom colony (May-stage phenology)
notes: If bloom has passed: document seed umbel for lifecycle content

shot_description: Emerging compound leaf cluster — mid-range showing plant emergence
notes: Pre-bloom (bloom June-August); compound leaf is the May identification feature

shot_description: Compound leaf close — showing ternate structure and toothed margin
Stem base detail — showing emerging leaves at ground level
Trillium follicle / seed pod stage — post-bloom May phenology
notes: Do not pick or disturb any trillium — photograph only

shot_description: Three-leaf whorl showing whorled arrangement — leaf detail
Wild Ginger colony — low angle showing heart-shaped leaves as lookalike species
notes: Lookalike documentation — Wild Ginger is a common companion and identification teaching point

shot_description: Leaf detail — showing hairy petiole and heart-shaped form
Canopy light quality — dappled light on forest floor (guide chapter opener use)
notes: No species ID required — atmospheric backdrop for guide layout

shot_description: Trail or path context — showing ecosystem access and setting
Understory composition — fern and moss on tree trunk
notes: Backdrop for guide cover or chapter header

shot_description: Lookalike comparison — photograph 1 common lookalike per target species encountered
notes: Document species name in notes; photograph any Poison Hemlock or other safety-relevant lookalike

shot_description: Backup / outtake pass — fill any sequence gaps from sessions above
notes: Review all previous shots before this pass; identify specific gaps

shot_description: Wide riparian habitat — creek bank and flow composition
surface_or_setting: Creek bank — Bent Creek / French Broad corridor
notes: Morning light 8-10 AM preferred. Check flood gauge before departing (USGS 03451500)

shot_description: Low-angle showing watercress leaves floating on moving water
notes: Rubber boots required for this shot — enter water margin carefully

shot_description: Leaf detail — pinnate form against water background
Elderberry wide habit — showing full shrub in riparian setting
notes: Late spring — likely in bloom stage (white flat-topped flower clusters)

shot_description: Flower cluster — close showing corymb structure
Cattail colony — wide showing emergent aquatic community
Pollen head detail — close showing cylindrical form
notes: May = pollen stage before brown sausage form develops

shot_description: Nettle patch at riparian edge — showing colony growth form
Leaf detail — toothed serrated margin showing opposite arrangement
notes: Do not touch leaves bare-handed

shot_description: Wild Bergamot in transition zone — bud stage if visible
notes: Late May Zone 5 — bud stage probable; full bloom July. Document bud for phenology content.

shot_description: Riparian bank composition — water surface and bank edge
Transition zone — wet meadow and shrub margin composition
Backup pass — any species encountered that are unexpected
notes: Document any unexpected edible species with species name in notes field

shot_description: Whole root on white card — showing forked taproot structure
license_type: CC-BY (prop — no image license needed for self-photographed)
notes: Photograph whole root first before cutting. Set white balance to auto; correct in post if needed.

shot_description: Root cross-section on white card — showing internal structure
notes: Cut cleanly with sharp knife; photograph within 10 minutes of cutting. Small amount of water spray can bring out texture.

shot_description: Root on dark linen — atmospheric lifestyle shot
notes: No scale reference in this frame — atmospheric use only (not identification)

shot_description: Scale reference shot — root alongside ruler
Whole dried root on white card — showing texture and color
notes: Photograph before cutting

shot_description: Cross-section — before misting (baseline color)
notes: Cut immediately before photographing; capture dry state first

shot_description: Cross-section — after misting (deepened yellow color) — MUST SHOOT WITHIN 5 MIN OF MISTING
notes: TIMER CRITICAL: mist root; set 5-minute phone timer; photograph before timer expires. Yellow color dulls as moisture evaporates. This is the hero image.

shot_description: Seed packet flat-lay on white card or wood surface
surface_or_setting: White foam board or wood plank
notes: Only if Strictly Medicinal package has arrived. Skip if not yet received — seeds are supplementary

shot_description: Dark linen atmospheric shot — heritage medicinal herb framing
Ramp leaves bundle on white card — showing reddish stem base
notes: Mist leaves lightly just before shooting. Refrigerate between setups. DO NOT let leaves wilt — reshoot if wilted.

shot_description: Single ramp leaf — close showing lance-shaped form and reddish base
Ramp bulb / bulblet showing elongated white form with reddish stem and papery skin
notes: Only if Prairie Moon bulblets received; use iNaturalist CC-BY if not available

shot_description: Dark linen atmospheric — leaves arranged showing spring harvest visual
Full plant habit — showing compound leaf structure and stem
surface_or_setting: Indirect light — north window or shaded table
notes: ONLY photograph if plant arrived by May 17 (48-hour acclimatization complete). If not yet received or acclimated: skip; use iNaturalist CC-BY fallback.

shot_description: Compound leaf detail — showing ternate division and serrated toothed margin
Compound leaf — second angle showing leaf undersurface (paler green)
Stem base showing emergence from soil and base structure
Emerging bud if present — close of any reproductive structure visible
notes: Only if flower bud visible on plant received. Document presence/absence in notes.

shot_description: Full plant habit — showing opposite-branched stem and leaf arrangement
Flower head — if blooming; showing lavender pom-pom-like flower cluster
notes: If not yet in bloom: document bud stage; full bloom will be available for iNaturalist CC-BY archive photo from July

shot_description: Leaf and stem detail — showing square stem and opposite leaves
notes: Square stem is a family identifier (Lamiaceae) — worth capturing as diagnostic feature

shot_description: Bud close — showing developing flower head structure
Combination flat-lay — ginseng root / goldenseal root / ramp leaf together
surface_or_setting: White foam board or wood surface
notes: Etsy listing thumbnail use — shows the scope of the bundle. Keep spacing clean.

shot_description: Combination lifestyle flat-lay on dark linen — heritage botanical framing
Kraft envelope with seed packet and root — educational kit composition
notes: Kraft seed envelopes from SHOOT_PROPS_CHECKLIST.md if available

shot_description: Scale reference verification — US quarter next to ginseng root
notes: Confirm quarter is legible in frame; used for editorial consistency across all root shots

shot_description: Scale reference verification — ruler next to goldenseal root cross-section
Review gaps from sequences above — re-shoot any missed frames
notes: Check shot log for any sequence position with 0 frames captured; fill gaps before ending session

shot_description: Lighting variation pass — try slightly different angle if any key shot is flat or unclear
notes: Rotate the specimen or move it relative to the light source rather than moving the light

shot_description: Final session check — import all frames; confirm no SD card or storage errors
qa_pass: Back up all captures to a second device before ending session. Do not rely on phone memory only.
